evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: ensure data quality, governance, and security standards; define BI architecture standards, principles and best practices; scalability, performance and enterprise alignment
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Classified as rigour because responsibilities centre on establishing 'BI architecture standards', 'data quality, governance, and security standards', and 'enterprise alignment' with no velocity or speed-to-ship signals.
evidence.domain_risk: IT sector enterprise BI architecture; no specific regulated sector named
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Classified as moderate because the JD specifies IT sector without naming a regulated industry; the enterprise context implies business decision impact without explicit regulatory or financial reporting consequence.
evidence.data_team_maturity: enterprise alignment; enterprise data modelling; 7+ years required; Microsoft Fabric platform
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Classified as mature because the role requires 7+ years, targets enterprise-scale Microsoft Fabric architecture, and references 'enterprise alignment' and governance tooling deployed at scale.
evidence.jd_authorship: design and deliver a modern Microsoft Fabric-based analytics platform; define lakehouse, data warehouse, and semantic modelling structures
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Classified as hiring_manager because specific technologies (Microsoft Fabric, Azure Data Factory, PySpark) and architectural deliverables (lakehouse, semantic modelling, curated data products) clearly describe the scope of work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: design and deliver a modern analytics platform; define BI architecture standards, principles and best practices
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Classified as internal_data because the primary output is the analytics platform itself — reusable data models, curated data products, and BI architecture standards serving internal data consumers.
evidence.autonomy_level: define BI architecture standards, principles and best practices; define lakehouse, data warehouse, and semantic modelling structures
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Classified as strategic because 'define BI architecture standards' and 'design end-to-end BI architecture' signal the candidate sets direction for the entire analytics platform, not just executes against a spec.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool usage or AI infrastructure enablement is mentioned anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: ensure data quality, governance, and security standards
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Classified as responsibility because 'ensure data quality, governance, and security standards' is an ownership verb framing data quality as a named accountability, not just a tool or skill.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: ensure data quality, governance, and security standards; enterprise alignment; data governance and quality practices
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Classified as high because 'ensure data quality, governance, and security standards' as a primary responsibility, combined with 'enterprise alignment', indicates trustworthiness and governance are central concerns dominating the role framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: design and deliver a modern Microsoft Fabric-based analytics platform
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Classified as greenfield because the mandate is to design and deliver a new Microsoft Fabric-based analytics platform from the ground up.
evidence.loss_aversion: ensure data quality, governance, and security standards — enterprise governance and trustworthiness are primary role framings, not secondary delivery concerns.
evidence.work_arrangement: Work Model: Hybrid (3 days onsite per week)
